name: Image generation and chart workflow
summary: Image and diagram generation workflow for deterministic diagrams, themed HTML/SVG technical diagrams, raster visual assets, image edits, and bounded PNG QA repair loops.
description: Use when generating or editing diagrams, charts, visual assets, posters, UI mockups, product images, infographics, academic figures, comics, avatars, storyboards, brand boards, or image-edit workflows, especially when exported PNGs need visual QA and bounded self-repair. Prefer deterministic Mermaid/SVG/HTML/canvas sources for text-heavy diagrams; use HTML technical diagrams for browser-ready system blueprints, architecture, deployment topology, agent runtime, memory flow, before-after architecture, workflow, sequence, data-flow, lifecycle, runbook, PII/data-lineage, and state-machine diagrams. Do not use for ordinary UI polish without generated imagery.

boundaryWorkflows: Use UI design for product-specific visual direction, brand tone, hierarchy, and visual QA of generated assets.
Use implementation-from-design when a Figma, Sketch, MasterGo, Pixso, screenshot, or other external design source is authoritative.
Use the HTML technical diagram route for browser-ready themed workflow, sequence, data-flow, lifecycle, runbook, PII/data-lineage, and state-machine diagrams.
Use draw.io studio instead when the priority is editable .drawio source, official shape lookup, or manual diagrams.net editing.
Use Canvas/Three.js or SVG animation workflows when the generated visual becomes an interactive renderer, shader, or motion asset rather than a static image.
